message:
test(core,docs): add lane-context override guard and tighten M4a IC doc wording

Two small follow-ups to the preceding statement-reuse-trace and
E3.2 primitive-selection doc commits.

## StatementReuseLaneOverrideGuard test helper

The statement-reuse trace captures its lane identity from
`FSQLITE_STATEMENT_REUSE_LANE_CONTEXT_OVERRIDE` under
`#[cfg(test)]` so unit tests can inject synthetic worker /
node ids without poking process-global env vars. That helper
was already threaded into `statement_reuse_lane_context()`,
but the test suite still had to manually `.with(|slot|
slot.replace(Some(...)))` at the start of every test and
manually clear it at the end, which made the
lane-override pattern verbose and leak-prone on panic.

Add a `StatementReuseLaneOverrideGuard` RAII helper in
`mod pager_routing_tests`:

- `new(worker_id, node_id)` captures the previous override
  via `slot.replace(Some(StatementReuseLaneContext { ... }))`
  and stores it in `self.previous` so nested overrides
  restore correctly.
- `Drop` puts the previous override back via
  `slot.replace(previous)`, which guarantees the thread-local
  is reset even if a test panics mid-assertion.

The guard is gated on `#[cfg(test)]` and lives alongside
`reset_statement_reuse_measurement` in the same test module
so future tests can write
`let _lane = StatementReuseLaneOverrideGuard::new("w1", "n1");`
at the top of their body and forget about cleanup.

## M4a IC column doc wording

The Inline-Critical summary in the E3.2 design doc still
said "M4 (ConcurrentRegistry) is the most promising
optimization target" but the actual classification landed in
M4a (active snapshot table) vs M4b (other ConcurrentRegistry
state). Update the bullet to say "M4a is the most promising
optimization target because it is the only one still using a
coarse Mutex where an immutable reader snapshot is viable",
which is the precise claim — M4b does not have the same
optimization path and was never the target.

The companion sentence is updated to say "M1, M2, M3, and the
M4 active snapshot table are the only metadata classes
touched inline-critical" instead of "M1, M2, M3, and M4", so
readers scanning for "what's in the publish window" get the
fine-grained answer.

files.patch:
@@ -499,10 +499,10 @@ class, establishing the coupling between Parts 1 and 2:
 | M8 SSI Evidence | — | — | Evidence recording | — |
 
 **Key insight:** The IC (INLINE-CRITICAL) column shows exactly what must be in
-the publish window. M1, M2, M3, and M4 are the only metadata classes touched
-inline-critical. Of these, M4 (ConcurrentRegistry) is the most promising
+the publish window. M1, M2, M3, and the M4 active snapshot table are the only
+metadata classes touched inline-critical. Of these, M4a is the most promising
 optimization target because it is the only one still using a coarse Mutex where
-a finer primitive is viable.
+an immutable reader snapshot is viable.
 
 ---
